disease.name: Image-Guided Biopsy Procedures
disease.note: Percutaneous image-guided biopsies using ultrasound, CT, or fluoroscopy. Core needle biopsy preferred for histology, FNA for cytology. Pre-procedure planning includes access route, risk assessment, and clotting parameters.

severityAssessment.severityLabel: If active bleeding/pneumothorax -> COMPLICATION; else -> NON-COMPLICATED

subtypes.name: CT-Guided Biopsy
subtypes.management: Requires breath-hold for lung/upper abdominal. Post-procedure CT for immediate complication assessment. Pneumothorax rate 10-30% for lung. Risk of hemorrhage for liver.

subtypes.name: Ultrasound-Guided Biopsy
subtypes.management: Preferred for liver, kidney, thyroid, breast, superficial lymph nodes, soft tissue. Real-time guidance of needle. Doppler to avoid vascular structures. Faster and less costly than CT.

subtypes.name: Fluoroscopy-Guided Biopsy
subtypes.management: Common for spine and bone biopsies. Often combined with CT guidance. Biopsy needle confirmed in AP and lateral planes.

imaging.name: Pre-procedure diagnostic CT/MRI/US
imaging.indication: target lesion characterization and access planning

imaging.name: Doppler ultrasound for vascular mapping
imaging.indication: to avoid vessels along needle path

management.protocolReference: ACR-SIR Practice Parameter for Image-Guided Percutaneous Biopsy

pathways.criteria: no coagulopathy, superficial target, easy access
pathways.treatment: Consent. Local anesthesia. US or CT guidance. Core or FNA biopsy. 16-20G needle. Minimal post-procedure observation (1-2 hours).

pathways.criteria: deep target (lung, liver), corrected mild coagulopathy, single antiplatelet held
pathways.treatment: Hold anticoagulation per guidelines. Bridging if needed. CT guidance preferred. Coaxial technique. Post-procedure observation (4-6 hours). Check imaging for complications.

pathways.criteria: uncorrectable coagulopathy, risky access, deep lesions near vessels
pathways.treatment: Multidisciplinary discussion. Consider alternative (EBUS, surgical biopsy). Correct coagulopathy if possible. Prep blood products. ICU bed post-procedure.
